mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP consistently drives equity valuations higher by expanding corporate earnings expectations. A positive GDPC1 growth rate predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in positive GDPC1 growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate below 5.0 percent signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 40 percent of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0 percent within a single month.

mechanism_narrative: The joint acceleration of housing starts and residential construction spending amplifies aggregate demand shocks. When both HOUST and RSAFS rise simultaneously over a three-month window, they predict positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between HOUST and RSAFS yields a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression of 60-day forward returns, or the joint signal produces a win rate below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, compressing equity valuations. A sustained increase in FEDFUNDS predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of instances where FEDFUNDS rises consecutively for three months.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain efficiency, which supports corporate profit margins. Positive INDPRO growth predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO growth against 60-day forward returns remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth exceeding 200,000 jobs per month indicates robust labor demand that fuels consumer spending and equity earnings. Sustained PAYEMS growth above this threshold predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The forward return distribution for months with PAYEMS > 200,000 is not statistically distinct from the overall sample distribution (Kolmogorov-Smirnov test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous decline in unemployment and acceleration in payroll employment confirms a self-reinforcing labor market recovery. This joint signal predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings revisions turn positive.
failure_condition: The interaction effect between UNRATE and PAYEMS on 5-day forward returns is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) or produces a negative directional coefficient in a rolling 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth stagnates while consumer price inflation accelerates, corporate margins face simultaneous demand destruction and cost-push pressure. This combination predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of instances where GDPC1 growth is below 1.5 percent and CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation exceeds 3.0 percent.
